name: KH-1 autophagy prediabetes trial is completed with no posted results
summary: NCT06445569 is directly relevant autophagy-induction registry context, but it is completed without posted results and cannot change the public evidence stage.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT06445569 as a completed 42-participant randomized KH-1 trial with insulin-resistance, autophagy, safety, inflammatory, cardiovascular, immune, and quality-of-life endpoints and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Non-diabetic adults with elevated blood glucose concentration
time_horizon: Three-month randomized period with longer-term six-month follow-up endpoints
quantitative_note: Actual enrollment is 42; registry status is completed; actual study completion was 2023-06-08; result status is not posted.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of efficacy or harm.
KH-1 does not yet have a normalized intervention record in the public map.
The primary endpoint is insulin resistance rather than a durable aging-functional endpoint.
